title: calamaria engelmannii
intro: Calamaria engelmannii, also known as Engelmann's calamaria, is a type of plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The health benefits of Calamaria engelmannii include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and leaves are typically harvested and dried for use in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are then used to treat various health conditions.
